response.justification: Mohammed Al-Said is a seasoned full-stack software developer with strong web, backend, and mobile engineering credentials, but the role demands deep expertise in SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator performance analysis, hardware-software co-design, and deep learning workload optimization — none of which appear anywhere in the resume. The candidate's closest relevant experience is low-latency systems work at Dunnhumby, which does not translate to the hardware architecture and silicon-level modeling this principal-level position requires.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software engineering experience across web, embedded, and systems domains, meeting the bachelor's+8yr experience threshold
Demonstrated performance-oriented thinking through low-latency, high-throughput service work (millions of HTTP requests/second) and asynchronous I/O optimization
Exposure to AI-agent infrastructure and MCP frameworks at re:cinq suggests some familiarity with the AI ecosystem
concerns: Zero evidence of SoC/IP modeling, SIMD/MIMD architecture, or any silicon-level hardware design work
No deep learning, neural network training/inference, or GPU workload analysis experience whatsoever
Skill set is almost entirely web/backend (TypeScript, Node.js, React, MongoDB) — fundamentally misaligned with a hardware performance architecture role
